featured.title: Azerbaijani Energy Firm Hit by Repeated Microsoft Exchange Exploitation
featured.summary:
A threat actor with affiliations to China has been linked to a "multi-wave intrusion" targeting an unnamed Azerbaijani oil and gas company between late December 2025 and late February 2026, marking an expansion of its targeting.
The activity has been attributed by Bitdefender with moderate-to-high confidence to a hacking group known as FamousSparrow (aka UAT-9244), which shares some level of
featured.suggestedTitle: Chinese APT Targets Azerbaijani Energy Sector
featured.suggestedSummary: A Chinese-affiliated threat actor, 'FamousSparrow', has been linked to multiple intrusions targeting an Azerbaijani energy firm. The group is exploiting vulnerabilities in Microsoft Exchange to gain access and persist within the network. This highlights the ongoing risk to critical infrastructure from state-sponsored actors, emphasizing the need for robust patch management and threat detection capabilities.

featured.title: New critical Exim mailer flaw allows remote code execution
featured.summary: A critical vulnerability affecting certain configurations of the Exim open-source mail transfer agent could be exploited by an unauthenticated remote attacker to execute arbitrary code. [...]
featured.reason: Critical RCE vulnerability in popular software
featured.suggestedTitle: Exim Mailer Vulnerability Allows Remote Code Execution
featured.suggestedSummary: A critical vulnerability in the Exim mail transfer agent could allow remote code execution when certain configurations are in place. With a publicly available proof-of-concept, threat actors could exploit this flaw to gain control over affected systems. Organizations using Exim should prioritize patching and review their configurations to mitigate potential exploitation risks.

quickNews.title: West Pharmaceutical says hackers stole data, encrypted systems
quickNews.summary: West Pharmaceutical Services disclosed that it was the target of a cyberattack that resulted in data exfiltration and system encryption. [...]

quickNews.title: Attackers Weaponize RubyGems for Data Dead Drops
quickNews.summary: Threat actors are publishing RubyGems packages that include scrapers targeting public-facing UK government servers, but with no clear objective.

quickNews.title: Microsoft's MDASH AI System Finds 16 Windows Flaws Fixed in Patch Tuesday
quickNews.summary:
Microsoft has unveiled a new multi-model artificial intelligence (AI)-driven system called MDASH to facilitate vulnerability discovery and remediation at scale, adding that it's being tested by some customers as part of a limited private preview.
MDASH, short for multi-model agentic scanning harness, is designed as a model-agnostic system that uses bespoke AI agents for different vulnerability

quickNews.title: Foxconn confirms cyberattack claimed by Nitrogen ransomware gang
quickNews.summary: Foxconn, the world's largest electronics manufacturer, says some of its North American factories are now working to resume normal operations after a cyberattack. [...]
